question: Write a C++ program that prompts the user to enter a C-style string (character array). Then, count and print the total number of vowels (a, e, i, o, u, case-insensitive) present in the string.

test_cases.expected: Enter a C-style string: Total number of vowels: 3
Enter a C-style string: Total number of vowels: 4
Enter a C-style string: Total number of vowels: 10
Enter a C-style string: Total number of vowels: 0
Enter a C-style string: Total number of vowels: 0
Enter a C-style string: Total number of vowels: 0
Enter a C-style string: Total number of vowels: 1

test_cases.input: This is a rather long string to test the buffer handling capability of the program. It should still count vowels correctly without overflowing.
test_cases.expected:
Enter a C-style string: Warning: Input was too long and might have been truncated.
Total number of vowels: 23
